data.title: Japan's Seven & i to set up holding company for non-core assets amid Couche-Tard bid
data.description:
Japan's Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) on Thursday unveiled plans to establish an intermediate holding company that will preside over the retailer's supermarket food business, specialty store and other businesses, and is planning to change its name.

The intermediate company will be tasked "with a major role of planning the corporate strategies, managing, and supporting" the non-core business, whose growth story differs from the convenience store business’s, the owner of 7-Eleven brand said in a release.

The initiative comes as Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (OTCPK:ANCTF) reportedly raised its acquisition offer for Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) to over $47 billion. The new proposal at $18.19 per share is more than 20% higher than Couche-Tard's (TSX:ATD:CA) previously rejected offer for the 7-Eleven owner sent last month.

Seven & i Holdings (OTCPK:SVNDY), which operates over 80,000 worldwide, has been pressured by investors to divest from its large portfolio of non-core businesses.

The holding company is expected to entail a total of 31 establishments, including the group's superstores business, general goods store Loft, baby goods store Akachan Honpo.

Seven & i will also be named York Holdings, to become an equity method affiliate by February 2025, with an initial public offering planned.

Shares of Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) eased 0.43% on the Japanese stock exchange on Thursday, after ending 4.7% higher in the previous session.

data.title: European markets mixed ahead of U.S. inflation numbers
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.07%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.33% Retail sales in Germany increased 1.6% M/M in August.

France (CAC:IND) -0.31% following modest gains in the previous session.

The annual consumer inflation rate in Norway rose to 3% in September.

The annual inflation in Denmark inched lower to 1.3% in September.

Industrial production in Austria dropped by 2.5% Y/Y in August.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic rose to 2.6% in September.

Industrial production in Slovakia rose by 0.9% Y/Y in August.

Industrial production in Sweden dropped by 3.2% Y/Y in August. Household spending in Sweden rose by 0.8% M/M in August. The Swedish economy expanded 1.1% M/M in August. Household spending in Sweden rose by 0.8% M/M in August.

The annual inflation rate in Hungary eased to 3% in September. The annual inflation rate in Hungary eased to 3% in September.

Industrial production in Turkey slipped by 5.3% Y/Y in August. The seasonally adjusted jobless rate in Turkey fell to 8.5% in August.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.09% lower, as traders were cautious ahead of the highly-anticipated US CPI report due later in the day. The data should offer further insights on how price pressures in the US economy are evolving, after the FOMC minutes reinforced the view the Fed will lower borrowing costs at a slower pace than initially expected.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the European front.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis points to 4.08%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.28%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.21%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Apple TV+ joins hands with Amazon's Prime Video to expand reach
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) entered a deal that will bring Apple TV+ under Amazon's Prime Video streaming service later this month for $9.99 per month in the U.S.

The deal is seen as a strategic effort by both companies to expand their reach, especially when the iPhone maker is lagging streaming giants like Netflix (NFLX), Disney+ (DIS), and Prime Video (AMZN).

Apple TV+ joins Prime Video’s add-on subscription options in the U.S., which include Warner Bros. (WBD) Discovery’s Max, Paramount Global’s (PARA) Paramount+, Sony’s (SONY) Crunchyroll, and Amazon-owned MGM+.

This development marks a notable shift for Apple (AAPL), which has traditionally bundled Apple TV+ with its other services like Apple Music and Apple Arcade.

The subscribers will gain access to popular Apple's (AAPL) original collection, including acclaimed series like Ted Lasso, The Morning Show, and Severance, as well as films and live sports events such as Major League Soccer matches.

“As we continue to expand our offering and make it easy for customers to personalize their streaming experience directly in one app, we are proud to welcome Apple TV+ and its popular and critically acclaimed shows, films, and events to Prime Video.” commented Mike Hopkins, Senior Vice President and Head of Prime Video and Amazon MGM Studios.

data.title: TD Bank said to face $3B penalties, asset cap in U.S. over money-laundering lapses
data.description:
Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE:TD) will likely face around $3B in penalties and restrictions on U.S. expansion as part of a settlement with regulators over failures to prevent money laundering, people familiar with the matter told the Wall Street Journal.

The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency is expected to fix an asset cap that will bar TD (TD) from growing beyond a certain level in the U.S. TD's U.S. unit will also plead guilty to criminal charges to resolve an investigation by the Department of Justice.

The DOJ and the Treasury Department's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network plan to appoint independent monitors to ensure the bank's compliance with the settlement. The FinCEN monitor is expected to be in place for four years.

The settlement may be announced as early as Thursday, and will include the DOJ, FinCEN, the OCC and the Federal Reserve. The DOJ is expected to receive the biggest portion of the penalties at around $1.8B, while FinCEN will likely be paid $1.3B.

The federal investigation began after agents found that Chinese criminal groups laundered millions of dollars in illegal fentanyl sales through TD's (TD) U.S. branches and even bribed the bank's employees.

TD (TD) has already set aside around $3B to account for the costs associated with the potential settlements with regulators.

data.title: Franklin Resources reports month-end AUM of $1.68 trillion
data.description:
• Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) on Wednesday reported preliminary month-end assets under management of $1.68 trillion at September 30, compared to $1.68 trillion at August 31, 2024.
  • Long-term net outflows of $22.4 billion, inclusive of $27.9 billion of long-term net outflows at Western Asset Management, were partially offset by the impact of positive markets, Franklin Resources (BEN) said.
  • For the quarter ended September 30, 2024, AUM reflected the positive impact of markets, partially offset by long-term net outflows of $31.3 billion, inclusive of $37.0 billion of long-term net outflows at Western Asset Management.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Corporación América Airports reports increase in domestic passenger fees in Argentina
data.description:
• Corporación América Airports S.A. (NYSE:CAAP) reported an increase in the domestic passenger fee from ARS2,540 to ARS5,685, for airports operated by its subsidiary, Aeropuertos Argentina 2000.
  • The new tariff applies to tickets issued from the date of the resolution's publication and for travel occurring after thirty calendar days from that date.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackstone Secured Lending prices offering of $400M 5.350% unsecured notes due 2028
data.description:
• Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (NYSE:BXSL) has priced an underwritten public offering of $400.0 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.350% notes due 2028, it said Wednesday after market.
  • The notes will mature on April 13, 2028 and may be redeemed in whole or in part at BXSL’s option at the applicable redemption price.
  • Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (BXSL) expects to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes among other things.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire raises $1.9B in yen-denominated bonds - report
data.description:
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) raised $1.9 billion through a seven-tranche yen-denominated bond deal, according to a report by Reuters that cites a term sheet.

This issuance marks the largest of its kind for the company since its initial foray into Samurai bonds in 2019.

The U.S. investment giant issued bonds with varying maturities of 3, 5, 7, 10, 20, 28, and 30 years. The largest tranche was the 3-year bond, raising about $1.04 billion, followed by the 5-year bond that garnered ~$0.39 billion.

During the transaction, longer-dated bonds were introduced while a proposed 15-year tranche was ultimately dropped, the deal's bookrunners showed, as per the report.

The final pricing for each tranche was set at the lower to middle end of the revised price guidance provided to investors.

Berkshire (BRK.A) indicated that the proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, although it did not disclose the size of the deal in the filing.

data.title: Redwood Trust prices reopening of $40M senior convertible notes
data.description:
• Redwood Trust (NYSE:RWT) priced ~$40M aggregate principal amount of its 7.75% convertible senior notes due 2027 in a private offering.
  • The notes will be issued as a reopening of, and will be part of the same series as, the 7.75% convertible senior notes due 2027 that the company originally issued in June 2022, of which $207.4M aggregate principal amount is currently outstanding.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 11, 2024.
  • Redwood (RWT) estimates that the net proceeds it will receive from the offering will be ~$38.2M.
  • Net proceeds will be used to repurchase a portion of the currently outstanding 5.75% exchangeable senior notes due 2025 previously issued by one of its subsidiaries.
  • The stock price traded marginally higher, up 1.2% on Wednesday during after hours of trade.

data.title: Dana gets new technology chief
data.description:
• Dana (NYSE:DAN) named Seth Metzger as senior VP and CTO, succeeding Christophe Dominiak who is retiring from Dana.
  • Mr. Metzger has been with Dana for more than two decades and has extensive experience in engineering and technology development.
  • He most recently served as senior VP of Electrification, as well as President of the Dana TM4 joint venture.
